response: The history of the internet began in the 1960s with US Department of Defense research, evolving from the 1969 ARPANET demonstration to the foundational adoption of TCP/IP protocols in 1983. The invention of the World Wide Web in 1989 and the 1993 launch of the Mosaic browser transitioned the technology from research institutions to the general public. Following the dot-com boom and the rise of mobile and cloud computing, the internet has become a ubiquitous force that has transformed global communication and commerce. Today, this digital infrastructure connects over 5 billion people and underpins nearly every aspect of modern life.
